methodology: When sources publish different numbers for the same gain, we show every source rather than picking one. The range itself is the finding. False precision in the positive direction is as much a distortion as false precision in the negative direction.
data_status: illustrative — pipeline not yet live; figures approximate published ranges as of mid-2026

disputes.name: People lifted from extreme poverty, 1990–2024

claims.methodology: Official World Bank international poverty line, 2017 PPP rebased
Aggregated across $2.15 and $3.65 lines; broader denominator
Argument that $2.15 line underestimates true poverty; at $7.40 the gain is far smaller

disputes.honest_finding: Direction is unambiguous: extreme poverty has fallen sharply by every line. The magnitude depends on where you draw the line — and the line is a value judgment, not just a measurement. We surface the lines and let the reader weigh.

disputes.name: Lives saved by COVID-19 vaccines, first year of rollout

claims.methodology: Mathematical model fit to excess-mortality data across 185 countries
Higher counterfactual mortality assumption; reported as a range with central estimate 19.8M

claims.note: WHO has not published a single point estimate
claims.methodology: References Watson et al. and Imperial as the reference range

disputes.honest_finding: Lives saved in the first year of rollout are credibly 14M–20M globally. The methodology disagreements concern the counterfactual outbreak path, not whether vaccines saved lives.

disputes.name: Reduction in under-5 deaths per year, 1990 vs. latest

claims.methodology: UNICEF/WHO/UN Population Division estimates; latest 2022 figures
GBD modeling with different demographic adjustments

claims.note: Earlier baseline estimates vary considerably; the 1990 figure itself is reconstructed

disputes.honest_finding: Roughly 7 million fewer children under five die each year now compared to 1990 — a population-scale gain larger than the death toll of most wars in history.

disputes.name: Share of global electricity from renewables, 2024

claims.methodology: Hydro + wind + solar + bio share of generation; reports 30%
Includes nuclear in 'low-carbon' framing; renewable-only figure 30%

claims.note: Capacity vs generation are different metrics — IRENA reports capacity, not generation share

disputes.honest_finding: Renewables supplied roughly 30% of global electricity generation in 2024. Including nuclear takes the low-carbon share over 40%.

disputes.name: Long-run trend in battle deaths per capita, 1950–2024

claims.methodology: Per-capita battle deaths from UCDP/PRIO and historical compilations

claims.note: Argues distribution is fat-tailed; declines are within statistical noise
claims.methodology: Power-law analysis of historical war casualties
Battle-related deaths in 2024 — higher than the 2010s low point
Visual emphasis on long downtrend with recent uptick

disputes.honest_finding: Per capita battle deaths today remain far below mid-20th century peaks, but recent years (Ukraine, Sudan, Gaza, Tigray) have lifted absolute figures above the 2010s. Calling the longer trend a 'permanent decline' is contested; the long-run direction is positive but conditional.
